title: Fix missing /symptom-checker route warning — remove standalone route, keep Symptom Checker in Health tab

requirements.text: Remove any standalone /symptom-checker route entry from the root router configuration (e.g., App.tsx or equivalent main router file) to eliminate the missing-route console warning. The Symptom Checker must not appear as a separate top-level route or stack screen.

quotes: Remove the /symptom-checker route reference from the root layout and navigation configuration to eliminate the warning.

acceptanceCriteria: No console warning about a missing /symptom-checker route when the app starts.
App.tsx (or equivalent router file) contains no <Route>, stack screen, or tab entry pointing to /symptom-checker.
No unused imports of a standalone SymptomChecker route component remain in the router file.

requirements.text: Ensure the Symptom Checker component continues to function correctly as an embedded component inside the Health tab's Digital Health section. It must not be accessible via a direct URL or navigation link outside of the Health tab.

quotes: Keep Symptom Checker Inside Health Tab
Health Tab → Digital Health Dashboard → Symptom Checker Component (internal component, not separate route)

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to the Health tab displays the Digital Health section with the Symptom Checker embedded and fully functional.
No broken component rendering or null-reference errors related to the Symptom Checker after route removal.
The bottom tab navigator contains exactly: Home, Bookings, Health, Subscriptions, Profile — with no extra tab for Symptom Checker.

requirements.text: Remove all orphaned imports, navigation links, and redirect logic that reference the removed /symptom-checker route across the entire frontend codebase (e.g., any useNavigate('/symptom-checker'), <Link to='/symptom-checker'>, or similar references in header, sidebar, or other navigation components).

quotes: Check and remove: Unused SymptomChecker route imports, Any navigation link pointing to /symptom-checker, Any redirect logic referencing the old route

acceptanceCriteria: A project-wide search for the string 'symptom-checker' (as a route path) returns no results in navigation or routing files.
No TypeScript/JavaScript import errors related to a removed SymptomChecker route module.
App compiles and runs with a clean console — no warnings or errors related to the symptom-checker route.

constraints: Do not remove or modify the Symptom Checker component itself — only remove its standalone route registration.
Do not alter any file listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
Do not change the bottom tab structure beyond removing the erroneous /symptom-checker entry.
nonGoals: Rebuilding or redesigning the Symptom Checker feature.
Adding new AI or health-assessment functionality.
Changes to backend routes or Motoko canister logic.
Any theme, color, or branding changes.
Adding a new standalone Symptom Checker page or module.
